input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown as a flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with a specific structure. This is a code generation/guidance request - I don't need to explore any codebase. Let me create the files directly.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - Dashboard (single page)
   - Products (with two sub-tabs: Active and Archived)
   - Settings page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

data.content:
Here's the structure and how it works:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — Three `FlyoutItem` entries:

| Flyout item | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single page, no tabs |
| **Products** | One `<Tab>` with two `<ShellContent>` children → renders as **Active** / **Archived** tab bar |
| **Settings** | Single page, no tabs |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a route so you can navigate to it from either products tab:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage));

// or pass a query parameter:
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Key points:**
- Pages in the visual hierarchy (the XAML) get routes automatically from their `Route` attribute.
- `ProductDetailsPage` is **not** in the flyout, so it must be registered via `Routing.RegisterRoute` — it will push onto the navigation stack over the current page.
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` enables lazy loading (pages are created only when first visited).
